assertion_results.text: Investigation identifies two independent root causes: (1) convention reference formatter using wrong case transform, (2) task creation using Feature issue type ID instead of Task (Steps 2-4)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md Step 4 explicitly identifies two root causes: 'Root Cause 1: Convention reference formatter uses wrong case transform' (line 88) describing kebab-case transform in shared/convention-utils.md, and 'Root Cause 2: Task creation uses Feature issue type ID instead of Task issue type ID' (line 99) describing plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a using Feature issue type ID 10142 instead of Task issue type ID 10050. Both are traced in Steps 2-3 and fully analyzed in Step 4.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard is triggered because the two root causes are in different modules/code paths (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md lines 110-112: 'Trigger: YES -- multiple independent root causes detected. The two root causes are in different modules (shared/convention-utils.md vs plan-feature/SKILL.md) and affect different code paths (convention formatting vs Jira issue creation).' The decomposition-guard.md file exists and is triggered, stating on line 1: 'Decomposition Guard -- ACME-502' and line 3: 'This bug appears to involve multiple independent issues'.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard presents both issues with their affected files/modules (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md presents both issues with affected files: item 1 'Convention reference formatter uses wrong case transform (in shared/convention-utils.md)' (line 5) and item 2 'Task creation uses Feature issue type instead of Task issue type (in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a)' (line 8). Each includes a description of the defect and its module location.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard offers the user two options: Proceed with single Task or Split into separate Bugs (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md lines 14-17 present two options: '1. **Proceed** -- create a single Task covering all fixes' and '2. **Split** -- I recommend creating separate Bugs for each independent issue, then triaging each one individually' followed by 'Choose (1/2):'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT silently create a single Task bundling both fixes — it waits for user input (Step 6 stop rule)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md ends with 'Choose (1/2):' on line 17, explicitly waiting for user input. No task creation output or Jira issue creation evidence exists in the outputs directory. The only two output files are investigation.md and decomposition-guard.md — no task was created. The workflow stopped at the decomposition guard as required.
